ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tracing_config" (
  "trace_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tracing configuration */,
  "trace_file" TEXT /* Filename for tracing output */,
  "trace_directory" TEXT /* Directory for tracing output */,
  "trace_level" INTEGER /* Level of client tracing */,
  "trace_file_append" INTEGER /* Value is true if tracing output should append to the existing trace file */,
  "trace_max_size" INTEGER /* Maximum size of the trace file before rolling over to a new file */,
  "trace_max_files" INTEGER /* Maximum number of trace files to keep */,
  "trace_format" TEXT /* Format of the trace output (e.g., JSON, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trace_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_config" (
  "security_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security configuration */,
  "security_mechanism" INTEGER /* Security mechanism for client connections */,
  "security_username" TEXT /* Username for user-only security */,
  "security_password" TEXT /* Password for password-based security */,
  "security_certificate" TEXT /* Path to the certificate file for certificate-based security */,
  "security_key" TEXT /* Path to the key file for certificate-based security */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("security_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the network client */,
  "client_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the network client */,
  "client_port" INTEGER /* Port number of the network client */,
  "trace_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the tracing configuration */,
  "security_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the security configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the configuration was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the configuration (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_accessed_at" TEXT /* Last time the configuration was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_trace_id FOREIGN KEY ("trace_id") REFERENCES tracing_config ("trace_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_security_id FOREIGN KEY ("security_id") REFERENCES security_config ("security_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, security officer) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the configuration */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client making the access request */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the access attempt (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configuration ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration being modified */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log (e.g., configuration change, security event) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Detailed message describing the log entry */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the log entry was generated */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client generating the log entry */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the log entry (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configuration ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the permission was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the permission was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the role-permission mapping was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the role-permission mapping was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)
